meta.title: Rezics Products | Rezics
meta.description: Explore the Rezics product family: catalog, web app, realms, wiki, shelves, and graph surfaces built on one shared work identity.

sections.title: Every surface starts from the same work identity.
sections.body: Catalog entries, reviews, shelves, tags, wiki pages, and realm discussions point back to shared Work Units instead of becoming separate databases.

sections.title: This site introduces the products; the app owns interaction.
sections.body: Search, saving, voting, editing, realm membership, wiki authoring, and other signed-in workflows stay in the main Rezics app.

storySections.title: Products can be split without splitting the graph.
storySections.body: Rezics Web, Realms, Wiki, Shelves, and Graph can become separate product surfaces while still sharing catalog identity, source facts, and community classification.

storySections.title: The list is allowed to show future-facing surfaces.
storySections.body: A planned status means the product name is directional, not a claim that the full workflow is already available.

products.name: Rezics Catalog
products.summary: The public cross-language index for works, aliases, sources, relationships, and media-specific metadata.
features: Shared Work Units for books, games, media, posts, and mixed works
Facts, aliases, source links, translations, and related works
One work identity across languages, platforms, editions, and communities

products.name: Rezics Web
products.summary: The main web app for browsing, reviewing, collecting, and contributing to the catalog.
features: Interactive search and work detail pages
Review, shelf, tag, and collection workflows
Signed-in contribution flows for catalog and community work

products.name: Rezics Realms
products.summary: Community spaces that classify, discuss, and curate works without forking the public catalog.
features: Feeds and portals around shared interests

products.name: Rezics Wiki
products.summary: Collaborative knowledge pages and realm wiki portals that keep context near the works and communities that need it.
features: Wiki posts for background, timelines, examples, and interpretation
Realm wiki zones for community-maintained context
Knowledge attached to works instead of scattered across tools

products.name: Rezics Shelves
products.summary: Curated paths through works, reviews, tags, realms, and other shelf items.
features: Explainable collections instead of opaque recommendation lists
Shelf items that can carry reviews and classification context
Reader and community curation around shared work identity

products.name: Rezics Graph
products.summary: The shared product graph behind catalog identity, source facts, community classification, and knowledge surfaces.
features: Work, tag, realm, shelf, review, and wiki relationships
Contract-first data shapes for product surfaces
A foundation for future Rezics tools without duplicating facts
